attacking_style: Excels in uphill sprint finishes (gradients 7-8.3%) and from breakaways, particularly when teammates create selection on uncategorized rises 12-15km out. Can threaten from 100km+ breakaways in Classics when race fractures early.
team_tactics: Trek-Segafredo/Lidl-Trek controls via hard pace over lumpy/mountain terrain to shed pure sprinters while preserving Pedersen. Uses aggressive pre-finish positioning and teammate tactical sacrifices to create final selections rather than traditional leadout trains.
terrain_preference: Gradient-finish sprints (7-8.3% ramps) and lumpy circuits where climbing capacity eliminates faster pure sprinters. Crosswind-susceptible stages where peloton splits early. Thrives when race difficulty creates reduced bunch sprints.
vulnerability: Mountain stages where cumulative climbing overwhelms sprint position (drops on sustained climbs, must regain contact). Form peaks mid-race rather than finale in Grand Tours, suggesting difficulty maintaining top-end sprint speed into final week.
key_patterns: Wins uphill sprints on 7-8.3% gradients where pure sprinters eliminated (4 observations)
Team uses aggressive pace over lumpy terrain to shed sprinters rather than leadout (5 observations)
Tactical flexibility: infiltrates mountain breakaways for points classification strategy (2 observations)
Survives lumpy/undulating stages better than faster sprinters via climbing capacity (6 observations)
Form improves through first two weeks of Grand Tours, peaks mid-race (3 observations)
Effective without traditional leadout on chaotic finishes, self-sufficient positioning (2 observations)
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 results show continued uphill sprint success (TDF stage win, Flanders 4th) but fewer observations than dominant 2022 Vuelta green jersey campaign suggest inconsistent deployment or race selection
